commit.message:
Fix ChatDatabase exception filter causing stuck sessions and unobserved task exceptions (#276)

## Problem
The 'FailedDelegation' session (3918 messages) got stuck because
`ChatDatabase.AddMessageAsync` threw an exception type not covered by
the catch filter. All ChatDatabase methods used:

```csharp
catch (Exception ex) when (ex is SQLiteException or IOException or UnauthorizedAccessException)
```

SQLite's async internals can throw `AggregateException`,
`InvalidOperationException`, `ObjectDisposedException`, etc. These
escaped the filter and became **unobserved task exceptions** from the `_
= _chatDb.AddMessageAsync(...)` fire-and-forget pattern used throughout
CopilotService.

## Fix
Broadened the catch filter in all 9 ChatDatabase methods from the narrow
3-type filter to `catch (Exception ex)`. All methods already:
- Return safe defaults (-1, false, 0, empty list)
- Log errors via `LogError()`
- Evict failed connections for recovery

## Tests Added
6 new regression tests in `ChatDatabaseResilienceTests.cs`:
- Corrupt DB file handling (4 tests) — triggers exception types outside
the original filter
- Fire-and-forget unobserved task exception verification — reproduces
the exact bug pattern

All 1931 tests pass (16/16 ChatDatabase tests pass).

feat(tiff): Support GPS fields, and other metadata enhancements (#5050)

Most visible change: The TIFF reader and writer now correctly handle GPS
tags.

Fixes #5049

But along the way, and to get it completely right, a fairly extensive
refactoring of our TIFF tag handling was needed. The libtiff behavior
around different tags is extremely convoluted, and there were a bunch of
cases we either didn't handle correctly, or were dropping on the floor.
So there are also some other non-GPS tags that we missed all along but
now read properly.

Note that the support for separate IFDs for GPS tags is only supported
via the API in libtiff 4.2 and newer, so this is disabled when bulding
against older libtiff versions. (Our support of old libtiff versions
extends extraordinarily far back in time.)

commit.message:
fix: increase TCP request buffer to 16MB for large transactions

Transactions consolidating many UTXOs can exceed 1MB when serialized
as hex. Increased safety limit from 1MB to 16MB and read buffer from
8KB to 64KB for faster large reads.

commit.message:
Improve conversion rate: fix false negatives, MCP resilience, goto/label support, new tools (#530)

Phase 3: Registry & Documentation Accuracy
- Remove 5 false-positive blockers from MigrationAnalyzer (stackalloc, volatile,
  extension-method, lock-statement, collection-expression) — all fully supported
- Fix calor-syntax-documentation.json IF block syntax inconsistencies
- Update TODO-converter-gaps.md: mark switch expressions, relational/compound
  patterns as DONE

Phase 4: MCP Server Resilience
- Add per-tool TimeoutSeconds property (default 60s, batch: 300s, compile: 120s)
- Use per-tool timeout in HandleToolsCallAsync instead of fixed 60s
- Nested try/catch in message loop prevents server becoming unresponsive

Phase 5: MCP Tooling Improvements
- New calor_batch_analyze: project-level convertibility with tiered scoring,
  blocker breakdown, and .calor-ignore/excludePatterns support
- New calor_roundtrip_check: C# to Calor to C# round-trip validation with diff
- New calor_get_example: browse self-test reference files as learning examples
- Add filePath parameter to calor_csharp_minimize for large files
- Extract GetStringArray helper to McpToolBase

Phase 6: Language Additions
- Add goto/label support (§GOTO{label}, §LABEL{name}): lexer, parser, AST,
  binder, emitter, converter, CalorEmitter — unblocks Newtonsoft parser files
- Remove goto/labeled-statement from MigrationAnalyzer blockers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: GitHub remote collaboration — SyncBar, TimelineSelector, snapshot search, E2E tests

- Add SyncBar component: remote URL, ahead/behind badges, Fetch/Push/Pull/Sync buttons, 5-min auto-fetch, conflict banner
- Add TimelineSelector: branch switcher dropdown with filter, create/delete, active indicator
- Add snapshot search/filter: magnifying glass toggle, filter bar, real-time node filtering
- Add quick-save: auto-generated contextual names (day + period + time), auto-select for override
- Add checkout_remote_branch: create local tracking branch from remote-only timeline
- Add publishTimeline store action for first-publish flow
- Add graph remote markers: green 'origin' badge on remote tip nodes via git2 refs scan
- Settings Repository tab: remote URL, auth method radio cards (gh CLI/PAT/SSH), git identity
- Auto-detect remote on project open, non-blocking background fetch
- Dirty guard: sync/pull blocked with user-facing error when unsaved changes
- Pull with fast-forward merge, PullResult::Diverged detection
- 19 Playwright E2E tests (phase0-snapshots, settings-remote, sync-bar, visual-check)
- 126 Rust tests, 68 Vitest tests — all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: redesign file changes feature

- File changes now show as a standalone item at the end of the assistant
  turn, not mid-content. All edits across multi-step turns are accumulated
  and flushed once via FileChangesSummaryItem.
- Files are grouped by path: 3 edits to 1 file = 1 entry with aggregated
  +/- stats.
- Per-file and global stats shown with green/red colored text using
  Brush.SuccessDefault and Brush.DangerDefault (avoids .caption class
  which overrides Foreground).
- Clicking a file opens full-file diff viewer with TextMate syntax
  highlighting, green-tinted change regions, line numbers, and prev/next
  navigation buttons.
- DiffView runs file I/O and change detection on background thread with
  loading ProgressBar indicator (yields via DispatcherPriority.Render).
- Handles \r\n vs \n line ending mismatch in change detection.
- Created files: added file_text extraction in ToolDisplayHelper, fallback
  line count from disk via EnsureStatsForCreatedFile().
- Inline Show diff button on tool calls still works, creates temporary
  FileChangeItem for the diff viewer.
- DiffShowRequested event signature changed from (string, string?, string?)
  to (FileChangeItem) throughout the chain.

feat: allow expanding bash command input while still executing (#274)

## Problem
When PolyPilot launches a command with a long command string (e.g., a
complex `bash` command), the full command text is truncated in the chat
UI. There is an expand/collapse feature for completed commands, but it
doesn't work for commands that are still executing — the chevron and
click handler are gated on `_hasOutput` which requires `IsComplete ==
true`.

## Fix

When expanded on a running command, the full tool input is shown below
the header in an `action-full-input` section. When expanded on a
completed command with output, the existing behavior (full input +
output) is preserved.

### Changes
- **`ChatMessageItem.razor`**: Add `_canExpand` variable; use it for
`expandable` CSS class, click handler, and chevron; add `else if` block
to show full input when expanded but no output yet
- **`ToolCallExpandTests.cs`**: 11 new tests mirroring the
expand/collapse logic to prevent regression

## Testing
- All 11 new tests pass
- All 1889 pre-existing tests pass (13 pre-existing PopupThemeTests
failures are unrelated)
- Mac Catalyst build succeeds

commit.message:
perf(kernels): add QuantizedAttentionWorkspace for scores buffer reuse [CPU] (#3271)

## Summary

Adds a workspace pattern for quantized attention, eliminating O(seq_len)
per-head allocation in the MHA/GQA hot loops.

### Problem
\quantized_dot_product_attention()\ allocates \ ec![0.0f32; seq_len *
seq_len]\ on every call. For MHA with \
um_heads=32\ and \seq_len=2048\, this is 32 16MB = **512MB of
unnecessary allocations** per forward pass.

### Solution
Same workspace pattern as PR #3266 (AttentionWorkspace for f32
attention):
- \QuantizedAttentionWorkspace\ holds a reusable \scores\ buffer
- \quantized_dot_product_attention_into()\ takes external scores buffer
- \quantized_multi_head_attention_with_workspace()\ reuses across heads
- \quantized_grouped_query_attention_with_workspace()\ same for GQA
- Existing functions delegate to \_into\ (backward compatible)

### Verification
- \cargo check -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features --features cpu\ 
- \cargo fmt\ 
- Backward compatible: no existing signatures changed

**Triage class: A (MERGE NOW)**

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
